aiAnalysis.keyChanges: This LCD covers superficial radiation therapy for nonmelanoma skin cancers and does not address molecular diagnostic testing
aiAnalysis.analysisNotes: This coverage determination is specific to superficial radiation therapy as a treatment modality for nonmelanoma skin cancers. It does not contain any provisions, criteria, or mentions related to molecular diagnostics, ctDNA testing, liquid biopsy, MRD monitoring, or tumor profiling. The document appears to be purely focused on radiation therapy coverage and would not impact molecular diagnostic test coverage decisions.

title: Natera: Financial assistance programs Program
summary: Qualified patients

data.title: Natera: Financial assistance programs Program
data.summary: Qualified patients

rawQuotes: Natera has robust financial assistance programs that can be offered to qualified patients to ensure that cost is not a barrier to testing
Natera accepts commercial and public insurances
Signatera™ is covered by Medicare for monitoring disease progression, disease recurrence, or relapse
Medicare patients diagnosed with solid tumors are fully covered if they meet one of these criteria: either recurrent, relapsed, refractory, metastatic, or advanced stages III or IV cancer
Natera is an in-network provider with most health plans, including Cigna and UnitedHealthcare. Natera also offers self-pay pricing and financial assistance options

title: Tempus: Financial Assistance Program Program
summary: Based on patient's household income and takes into account relevant life circumstances, such as medical debt or loss of income due to a patient's diagnosis

data.summary: Based on patient's household income and takes into account relevant life circumstances, such as medical debt or loss of income due to a patient's diagnosis

papProgram.name: Financial Assistance Program
papProgram.eligibility: Based on patient's household income and takes into account relevant life circumstances, such as medical debt or loss of income due to a patient's diagnosis

copayAssistance.notes: No specific copay assistance program mentioned

rawQuotes: Tempus accepts all insurance plans. Your out-of-pocket costs may vary depending on your plan benefits.
Through our financial assistance program, we help provide access to our tests and services to patients in financial need.
Approval is based on a patient's household income and takes into account relevant life circumstances, such as medical debt or loss of income due to a patient's diagnosis.
Payment must be made prior to testing. The test will be canceled if Tempus does not receive payment.
Online & Phone Applicants: You will receive an application decision at the time of submission.
Our Customer Support Team is available from 8:00 am CT – 5:00 pm CT, Monday through Friday at 800-739-4137

title: Aetna: Tumor Markers (CPB 0352) Updated
summary: This Aetna policy document primarily denies coverage for ctDNA, liquid biopsy, and MRD testing approaches, with the content focusing on traditional tumor markers rather than circulating tumor DNA technologies. The policy lists multiple named tests (signatera, guardant-reveal, foundationone-liquid, etc.) and MRD billing codes but appears to classify them as not medically necessary.

data.title: Aetna: Tumor Markers (CPB 0352) Updated
data.summary: This Aetna policy document primarily denies coverage for ctDNA, liquid biopsy, and MRD testing approaches, with the content focusing on traditional tumor markers rather than circulating tumor DNA technologies. The policy lists multiple named tests (signatera, guardant-reveal, foundationone-liquid, etc.) and MRD billing codes but appears to classify them as not medically necessary.

analysis.summary: This Aetna policy document primarily denies coverage for ctDNA, liquid biopsy, and MRD testing approaches, with the content focusing on traditional tumor markers rather than circulating tumor DNA technologies. The policy lists multiple named tests (signatera, guardant-reveal, foundationone-liquid, etc.) and MRD billing codes but appears to classify them as not medically necessary.
keyConditions: Policy focuses on traditional tumor markers and somatic mutations in oncology
Covers specific biomarkers like ALK, BRAF, BCR/ABL for targeted cancer types
Requires medical necessity criteria to be met for covered tumor markers
Excludes germline mutations (refers to separate policy CPB 0140)
Covers some circulating biomarkers like AR-V7 in CTCs for specific prostate cancer cases

title: UnitedHealthcare: Molecular Oncology Testing for Cancer (Solid Tumors) Updated
summary: UnitedHealthcare denies coverage for most ctDNA and liquid biopsy tests, explicitly listing MRD assays (including Signatera, Guardant Reveal, RaDaR, Haystack MRD) and multi-cancer early detection tests (like Galleri) as unproven and not medically necessary. Limited coverage is provided only for liquid biopsy multigene profiling (≤50 genes) specifically for non-small cell lung cancer.

data.title: UnitedHealthcare: Molecular Oncology Testing for Cancer (Solid Tumors) Updated
data.summary: UnitedHealthcare denies coverage for most ctDNA and liquid biopsy tests, explicitly listing MRD assays (including Signatera, Guardant Reveal, RaDaR, Haystack MRD) and multi-cancer early detection tests (like Galleri) as unproven and not medically necessary. Limited coverage is provided only for liquid biopsy multigene profiling (≤50 genes) specifically for non-small cell lung cancer.

analysis.summary: UnitedHealthcare denies coverage for most ctDNA and liquid biopsy tests, explicitly listing MRD assays (including Signatera, Guardant Reveal, RaDaR, Haystack MRD) and multi-cancer early detection tests (like Galleri) as unproven and not medically necessary. Limited coverage is provided only for liquid biopsy multigene profiling (≤50 genes) specifically for non-small cell lung cancer.

Liquid biopsy multigene profiling limited to ≤50 genes for NSCLC only
NGS panels >50 genes or CGP generally not covered unless otherwise specified

title: UnitedHealthcare: Molecular Oncology Testing for Hematologic Cancer Diagnosis Updated
summary: This policy focuses on hematologic cancer testing and explicitly denies coverage for molecular tests other than clonoSEQ for MRD assessment. While it covers clonoSEQ for MRD testing in specific blood cancers, it restricts other molecular oncology tests and references a separate policy for solid tumor testing.

title: EviCore (Evernorth): Multi-Cancer Early Detection Screening (MOL.TS.396.A) Updated
summary: EviCore denies coverage for multi-cancer early detection (MCED) screening tests, classifying them as experimental, investigational, or unproven due to insufficient evidence of clinical utility. The policy specifically states there is no evidence supporting ctDNA assays for cancer screening outside of clinical trials.

analysis.summary: EviCore denies coverage for multi-cancer early detection (MCED) screening tests, classifying them as experimental, investigational, or unproven due to insufficient evidence of clinical utility. title: Carelon (formerly AIM Specialty Health): Genetic Liquid Biopsy in Cancer Management - Landing Page Updated
summary: Carelon has established clinical appropriateness guidelines for genetic liquid biopsy testing in cancer management and surveillance, effective November 15, 2025, with updated codes as of January 1, 2026. The policy appears to take a restrictive stance, requiring specific clinical criteria to be met and stating that genetic tests not specifically mentioned in the guidelines are considered not medically necessary.

data.title: Carelon (formerly AIM Specialty Health): Genetic Liquid Biopsy in Cancer Management - Landing Page Updated

metadata.policyName: Genetic Liquid Biopsy in Cancer Management - Landing Page

analysis.summary: Carelon has established clinical appropriateness guidelines for genetic liquid biopsy testing in cancer management and surveillance, effective November 15, 2025, with updated codes as of January 1, 2026. The policy appears to take a restrictive stance, requiring specific clinical criteria to be met and stating that genetic tests not specifically mentioned in the guidelines are considered not medically necessary.
keyConditions: Genomic testing must have established analytical and clinical validity
Must be performed in an appropriately certified laboratory
Testing limited to evaluation following an intervention or when there is a change in clinical status
Must demonstrate reasonable likelihood that intervention will change management and/or lead to improved patient outcomes
Genetic tests not specifically mentioned in guidelines are considered not medically necessary
May require peer-to-peer conversation for appropriateness determination

title: BCBS Michigan: Circulating Tumor DNA Updated
summary: BCBS Michigan covers ctDNA/liquid biopsy testing only for selecting targeted therapy in advanced solid cancers when specific criteria are met, but denies coverage for MRD testing and cancer screening applications including the Galleri test. Coverage requires advanced/metastatic cancer, inability to perform tissue testing or FDA-approved companion diagnostic status, and no prior testing with the same panel.

title: Highmark BCBS: NavDx HPV ctDNA Testing (L-267) Updated

title: Blue Cross NC: Liquid Biopsy (AHS-G2054) Updated
summary: Blue Cross NC provides coverage for liquid biopsy/ctDNA testing in specific clinical scenarios for NSCLC (when tissue testing is inadequate), HR-positive/HER2-negative breast cancer (PIK3CA testing), and castration-resistant prostate cancer (AR-V7, BRCA1/2). However, screening tests like Galleri and most other liquid biopsy applications are considered investigational and not covered.

analysis.summary: This document is an index page for BCBS Minnesota's Lab Management Guidelines administered through eviCore, but does not contain the actual clinical guidelines or coverage policies. No specific coverage information for ctDNA, liquid biopsy, or MRD testing can be determined from this navigation page.
significantChanges: The document notes that EviCore.com recently upgraded and some bookmarked Clinical Guidelines might have changed, suggesting users should verify they are accessing the most recent guidelines

title: BlueCross BlueShield South Carolina: Liquid Biopsy Updated
summary: BlueCross BlueShield South Carolina provides limited coverage for ctDNA/liquid biopsy testing in specific clinical scenarios for NSCLC, advanced breast cancer, and castration-resistant prostate cancer, but denies coverage for screening applications and most other uses. The policy explicitly states that liquid biopsy testing for screening (including GRAIL Galleri) and most other malignancy detection/monitoring is considered not medically necessary.

data.title: BlueCross BlueShield South Carolina: Liquid Biopsy Updated
data.summary: BlueCross BlueShield South Carolina provides limited coverage for ctDNA/liquid biopsy testing in specific clinical scenarios for NSCLC, advanced breast cancer, and castration-resistant prostate cancer, but denies coverage for screening applications and most other uses. The policy explicitly states that liquid biopsy testing for screening (including GRAIL Galleri) and most other malignancy detection/monitoring is considered not medically necessary.

analysis.summary: BlueCross BlueShield South Carolina provides limited coverage for ctDNA/liquid biopsy testing in specific clinical scenarios for NSCLC, advanced breast cancer, and castration-resistant prostate cancer, but denies coverage for screening applications and most other uses. The policy explicitly states that liquid biopsy testing for screening (including GRAIL Galleri) and most other malignancy detection/monitoring is considered not medically necessary.
keyConditions: NSCLC: Coverage when tissue-based testing is infeasible, insufficient tissue available, incomplete biomarker assessment, or concurrent with tissue testing
Advanced breast cancer (recurrent/unresectable or stage IV): Coverage for specific mutations (AKT1, ESR1, PIK3CA, NTRK, RET, PTEN) when concurrent with tissue testing or tissue testing was negative
Castration-resistant prostate cancer: Coverage for AR-V7 and BRCA1/BRCA2 testing for therapy selection
Intermediate-risk microhematuria: Coverage for Cxbladder Triage with specific patient acknowledgment conditions
Annual testing allowed for qualifying individuals
testsExplicitlyMentioned: GRAIL Galleri - explicitly denied for screening
Cxbladder Triage - covered for specific intermediate-risk microhematuria cases
CELLSEARCH CTC - explicitly denied
Various urine-based tests (AssureMDx, Bladder CARE, etc.) - explicitly denied
significantChanges: Effective date 2026-01-01 suggests this is a future policy update
Specific coverage criteria established for breast cancer and prostate cancer ctDNA testing
Explicit denial of screening applications including multi-cancer early detection tests

title: Regence BCBS (OR/WA/UT/ID): Circulating Tumor DNA and Circulating Tumor Cells (LAB46) Updated
summary: Regence BCBS restricts coverage for ctDNA/liquid biopsy tests to very limited targeted treatment selection scenarios, primarily for advanced/metastatic breast cancer or when tissue testing is not feasible. All MRD testing and cancer screening applications are explicitly considered investigational and not covered.

metadata.policyName: Circulating Tumor DNA and Circulating Tumor Cells (LAB46)

analysis.summary: Regence BCBS restricts coverage for ctDNA/liquid biopsy tests to very limited targeted treatment selection scenarios, primarily for advanced/metastatic breast cancer or when tissue testing is not feasible. All MRD testing and cancer screening applications are explicitly considered investigational and not covered.
keyConditions: Advanced or metastatic breast cancer that is ER-positive and HER2-negative
Tissue-based testing cannot be performed due to insufficient sample or inaccessible tumor
Test must include genes with FDA-approved targeted therapy for the specific cancer indication
Limited to targeted treatment selection only
testsExplicitlyMentioned: Guardant360 CDx - covered for limited targeted treatment selection criteria
Signatera - listed as investigational for MRD testing
Galleri - listed as investigational for cancer screening
significantChanges: Policy effective January 1, 2026 suggests this is a new or updated policy
Explicit exclusion of MRD testing as investigational
Comprehensive list of investigational tests including named MRD and screening tests

title: WellCare (Centene): Oncology: Circulating Tumor DNA and Circulating Tumor Cells (Liquid Biopsy) Updated
summary: WellCare provides coverage for ctDNA/liquid biopsy testing but with significant restrictions to specific advanced cancer types and clinical scenarios. Broad molecular profiling panels are covered for stage IV/metastatic cancers including lung, pancreatic, gastric, prostate, melanoma, colorectal, and several other advanced malignancies, while single gene tests have more limited indications.

data.title: WellCare (Centene): Oncology: Circulating Tumor DNA and Circulating Tumor Cells (Liquid Biopsy) Updated
data.summary: WellCare provides coverage for ctDNA/liquid biopsy testing but with significant restrictions to specific advanced cancer types and clinical scenarios. Broad molecular profiling panels are covered for stage IV/metastatic cancers including lung, pancreatic, gastric, prostate, melanoma, colorectal, and several other advanced malignancies, while single gene tests have more limited indications.

metadata.policyName: Oncology: Circulating Tumor DNA and Circulating Tumor Cells (Liquid Biopsy)

analysis.summary: WellCare provides coverage for ctDNA/liquid biopsy testing but with significant restrictions to specific advanced cancer types and clinical scenarios. Broad molecular profiling panels are covered for stage IV/metastatic cancers including lung, pancreatic, gastric, prostate, melanoma, colorectal, and several other advanced malignancies, while single gene tests have more limited indications.
keyConditions: Advanced or metastatic cancer diagnosis required (Stage IV, locally advanced, unresectable, or recurrent)
Specific cancer types only: lung adenocarcinoma, pancreatic, gastric, esophageal, prostate, melanoma, colorectal, cervical, biliary tract, ovarian, breast cancers
Simultaneous tissue and liquid biopsy testing only allowed for lung cancers
Single gene tests limited to specific scenarios (e.g., EGFR testing when tyrosine kinase inhibitor therapy being considered)
Tests considered investigational for all other indications not explicitly listed
testsExplicitlyMentioned: FoundationOne Liquid CDx - covered for specified advanced cancers
Guardant360 CDx and 83+ genes - covered for specified advanced cancers
Tempus xF - covered for specified advanced cancers
significantChanges: Document version V1.2025 with last revision 11/2024 suggests recent updates
Expanded list of covered cancer types beyond traditional lung cancer focus
Specific restriction that simultaneous tissue and liquid biopsy only allowed for lung cancers

title: Kaiser Permanente: Genetic Screening and Testing Clinical Review Criteria Updated
summary: Kaiser Permanente's policy provides coverage for specific FDA-approved ctDNA/liquid biopsy tests for Medicare members through CMS guidelines, including FoundationOne Liquid CDx and Guardant360 CDx. For non-Medicare members, the policy appears restrictive with limited guidance on liquid biopsy coverage outside of advanced cancer NGS testing.

data.title: Kaiser Permanente: Genetic Screening and Testing Clinical Review Criteria Updated
data.summary: Kaiser Permanente's policy provides coverage for specific FDA-approved ctDNA/liquid biopsy tests for Medicare members through CMS guidelines, including FoundationOne Liquid CDx and Guardant360 CDx. For non-Medicare members, the policy appears restrictive with limited guidance on liquid biopsy coverage outside of advanced cancer NGS testing.

metadata.policyName: Genetic Screening and Testing Clinical Review Criteria

analysis.summary: Kaiser Permanente's policy provides coverage for specific FDA-approved ctDNA/liquid biopsy tests for Medicare members through CMS guidelines, including FoundationOne Liquid CDx and Guardant360 CDx. For non-Medicare members, the policy appears restrictive with limited guidance on liquid biopsy coverage outside of advanced cancer NGS testing.
keyConditions: Must meet CMS National Coverage Determinations for Medicare members
Non-Medicare members must use preferred labs (Prevention, Invitae/LabCorp) when available
Advanced cancer NGS testing allowed at specified labs (CellNetix, Oncoplex, Caris Life Sciences)
Must meet general genetic testing criteria including clinical risk, scientific validity, and direct impact on clinical management
testsExplicitlyMentioned: FoundationOne Liquid CDx (0239U) - covered for Medicare under CMS guidelines
Guardant360 CDx (0242U) - covered for Medicare under CMS guidelines
Galleri - mentioned in pre-extracted data but no explicit coverage decision in document
significantChanges: Document effective 2022-01-01 with recent CMS updates through 08/21/2025
Addition of newer CDx codes including oncoReveal CDx (0523U) effective 1/1/2025
Broad spectrum tumor molecular profiling listed as having insufficient evidence
